notes: RfaH-CTD is described as switching from an alpha-helical hairpin to a beta-barrel-like translation-factor state.
The holdout is opened only after the sealed packet hash exists.
The N-terminal domain is treated as an active contributor to the RfaH fold-switch mechanism.
Recent work still tests interdomain residues controlling the RfaH fold switch.
sources.source_type: post-seal interdomain residue and transition-mechanism holdout

notes: RfaH is linked to transcription antitermination and translation activation/coupling contexts.
sources.source_url_or_citation: UniProt P0AFW0 GO/function evidence including PMID 22817892 and transcription/translation coupling reviews
